article.description: — Cade Cunningham scored 28 points, Paul Reed had 16 of his 22 in the first quarter and the Detroit Pistons beat the Toronto Raptors 113-95 on Wednesday night in the final game before the All-Star break for both teams.
article.headline: Cunningham scores 28 points, Pistons overcome suspensions to rout Raptors 113-95

images.caption: Cade Cunningham tallies 28 points as the Pistons rout the Raptors.
images.name: Cade Cunningham leads all scorers in Pistons' win

article.linkText: Cunningham scores 28 points, Pistons overcome suspensions to rout Raptors 113-95
article.story: TORONTO -- — Cade Cunningham scored 28 points, Paul Reed had 16 of his 22 in the first quarter and the Detroit Pistons beat the Toronto Raptors 113-95 on Wednesday night in the final game before the All-Star break for both teams. The Eastern Conference-leading Pistons won for the sixth time in seven games despite playing without centers Isaiah Stewart and Jalen Duren. The two began serving suspensions handed down earlier in the day for their roles in a fight Monday in a win at Charlotte. Duncan Robinson scored 13 points and Tobias Harris added 12 points and 11 rebounds as the Pistons improved to 18-7 on the road. Detroit never trailed in posting its seventh consecutive victory over Toronto. Toronto’s Immanuel Quickley scored 18 points and Scottie Barnes added 17 . RJ Barrett had 16 points and Brandon Ingram 13. The Pistons outscored the Raptors 50-34 on points in the paint and had 46 rebounds to Toronto’s 35. Cunningham finished with seven rebounds and nine assists, and matched a season-high by making six of 11 attempts from 3-point range. His career-high is seven, set last season. Making his seventh start, Reed opened the scoring with a 3-pointer and connected on all seven of his attempts in a high-scoring first. Toronto center Jakob Poeltl returned after missing 24 games because of a sore lower back. He had nine points and six rebounds in 20 minutes. Up next Pistons: At New York on Thursday, Feb. 19. Raptors: At Chicago on Thursday, Feb. 19. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba

images.name: Luka Doncic [1296x729]

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.caption: Led by Victor Wembanyama, the Spurs continue to see their NBA championship odds shorten.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.
images.name: Jaylen Brown [1296x729]

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers on December 29, 2025 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
